California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
July 31, I99 0
Mike Dohrney
office of the city Attorney
1500 warburton Avenue
santa c1ara, cA 95050
. 
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. I-9 0-3 59
Dear !1r. Downey:
You have requested advice concerni.ng the resPonsibilities of
varj,ous cities and the County of Santa C1ara under the conflict of
interest provisions of the potitical 
Reform Act (the nA";rr1.1/
Your question originated from telephone conversations \.rith
Margaret Ellison of our staff and rnyself which was later followed
up with a fax of a joint powers agreement to enable us to nore
adequately respond to the verbal guestion provided on the
telephone. Since you have not, by letter, specifically requested
advice, ree consider this to be a request for infornal
assistance. 2 /
OUESTION
Is a public benefit study group comprised of the county of
Santa clara and the cities located within the county of santa
clara subject to the disclosure and disqualification provisions of
the Political Reforn Act?
l/ 
covernment Code sections 81ooo-91015. Al.L statutory
references are to the covernment Code un.Less otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 Catifornia Code of Regulations
section 18000, et se(I. A11 references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/ Informal assistance does not
imrnunity provided by an opinion
(covernment Code Section 83114;
1832e (c) (3) . )
provide the requestor with the
or formaL written advice.
2 Cal. code of Regs. Section
428 J Street, Suite 80O o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322-5660


Page Two
」u■y 3■′ ■990
CONCLUSTON
ft appears that the publ,ic benefit study group is solely
advisory in nature and is not subject to the disclosure and
disgual ificatj-on provisions of the Act.
FACTS
on the tel-ephone you indicated that the county of Santa clara
and various cities within the county have entered into a joint
powers agreement to conduct a public benefit study on a proposed
sports stadium facility. 
The group will be addressing such issues
as the econornic irnpact of such a stadium, the cost benefit to the
community, the amount of financial participation by respective
cities, etc. 
The city and county representatives wiLl be
reporting to their respective jurisdictions lrith the findings of
the study and may be naking recommendations to their respective
legislative bodies regarding specific plans to improve the public
benefit of such a stadium.
The decision to proceed wj.th the stadium project does not
rest with each legislative body but rather will be a ballot
measure to be decided upon by the voters j.n Santa clara county at
the November 1990 election.
During our phone conversation you hrere asked to send a copy
of the joint powers agreernent so we could better address this
issue. That agreement provides that the parties to the agreement
"...join 
together for the purpose of creating recommendations
pertaining to: 
(a) whether or not a facility 
should be
constructed in Santa Clara County (City of Santa Clara) through a
benefit study which wiLt identify specific benefits to each city
and genera.L benefits to the county as a wholei and (b) any other
area of inquiry related to such potential facilities 
deerned
appropriate by the parties hereto...rr The agreement continues to
provide that "...the first task of the governing board shall be to
identify the site for the facility.'t
You indicated to me on the tel,ephone that the site for the
facility 
was previously deternined as land owned by the County of
Santa clara and that there is no other site within the county
which could accomrnodate such a stadiurn.
ANALYS IS
The Political Reforn Act requires every agency to adopt and
pronulgate a conflict of interest code (Section 87aoo). A-
conflict of interest code is a docunent which designaies the
positions within the agency which rnake or participite in the


Page Three
July 31, 1990
naking of decisions which nay foreseeably have a material effect
on any financial interest and further sets forth the types of
investments, busj-ness positj-ons, income and real property made
reportable by those designated employees (Section a73o2).
The term ttdesignated enployeert excludes rr. .
nerflber of any board or cornmission which serves a
functj-on.'r (Section a2oj-9, emphasis added.)
.any unsalaried
solely advisorv
Commission Regnllation l-8700 provides guidance in deternining
whether a board or commission is rrsolely advisory" or has
dec i s ion-rnaking authority.
(a) 'izublic official 
at any Level of state or loca1
governmentri neans every natural, Person who is a member,
officer, 
employee or consultant of a state or loca1
government agency.
(1) 
nMembertr shall include, but not be lirnited to,
salaried or unsal-aried members of board or
cornmissions with decision-making authority. 
A
board or commj-ssj.on possesses decision-making
authority whenever:
(A) It may make a final governmental' decision;
(B) It nay corupel a governmental- decision; or it
may prevent a governlnental decision either by
reason of an excLusive power to initiate 
the
decision or by reason of a veto which may not be
overridden; or
(c) It makes substantive recommendations whi-ch
are, and over an extended period of time have been,
regularly approved without significant amendment or
rnodification by another public official 
or
governmentaL agency.
Regulation 18700 (a) (1)
The stadiurn study group does not nake, compel or prevent any
final, decisions. It is nerely obtaining statistical 
information
on the probable benefits the respective jurisdictions 
rnay enjoy
should the stadiurn issue pass the November ba1Iot.
The study group is at present solely advisory in nature.
This advice may change should the measure pass and the group
continue in existence with different functions or obligations.


Page Four
July 31, 1990
I hope this has
you have any furtfrer
(e15) 322-590r,.
answered your question satisfactorily. 
If
guestions, please feeL free to call me at
S incerel y ,
Scott Hal-labrin
PoIitical Reform consultant
Legal Division
sH/rET/)t
